notes.anchor: all the way up to the Piscina Publica
notes.note: The Piscina Publica, a public reservoir in southern Rome near the Aventine that gave its name to the surrounding district. Its mention marks how far the Tiber flood of 54 BC reached.

notes.anchor: those who in the assembly judge crooked dooms by violence
notes.note: A quotation of Homer (Iliad 16.387-388) on men who pervert justice and provoke Zeus's flooding wrath. Cicero applies it bitterly to the corrupt jurors who acquitted Gabinius, reading the real flood as the gods' answer.

notes.anchor: most of all about the dictatorship
notes.note: The persistent rumour of 54 BC that Pompey would be made dictator to end the electoral deadlock and disorder. Cicero promises a fuller report once he is back in Rome.
A small wooden candelabrum (lychnuchus) that Cicero believed Quintus had had made on Samos. The homely detail dates the letter to the pre-dawn hours and signals affection for his absent brother.
